line_items.description:
Web Hosting 1 year Dec 1, 2025 - Dec 1, 2026
(Recurring Yearly Fee, next invoice sends Nov 1, 2026)

Stack:
https://astro.build/  framework
https://railway.com/  hosting
https://supabase.com/  database
https://resend.com/  email
https://cloudflare.com/turnstile  form captcha

# Rothco LLC Website Rebuild - Project Scope & Deliverables

## Project Overview
Complete migration and rebuild of rothcollc.com from Webflow to Astro JS framework, including full functionality restoration, form processing, and deployment setup.

---

## 1. Site Migration & Conversion

### 1.1 Webflow to Astro JS Conversion
- Converted entire website from Webflow platform to Astro JS framework
- Migrated from proprietary Webflow CMS to modern static site generator
- Preserved all original design, styling, and visual elements
- Maintained responsive design and mobile compatibility

### 1.2 Static Asset Migration
- Downloaded and migrated 226+ files (~89MB) from original Webflow site
- Organized all CSS, JavaScript, images, and fonts into local project structure
- Converted all asset paths from Webflow CDN to local/public directory structure
- Preserved all Webflow-specific classes and data attributes for styling compatibility

### 1.3 HTML to Astro Component Conversion
- Converted all 9 HTML pages to Astro component format:
  - Homepage (index.astro)
  - About Us page
  - Projects listing page
  - 4 individual project detail pages (Quincy Addition, VAMC Campus Electrical, VAMC Worcester Airport Roof, Melrose Home)
  - Services page
  - Acquisitions page
  - Join Our Team page
  - Contact page
- Extracted and modularized reusable components (Header, Footer)
- Created BaseLayout component for consistent page structure

---

## 2. Component Architecture

### 2.1 Reusable Components
- **Header Component**: Navigation menu with active page highlighting, mobile menu functionality
- **Footer Component**: Site footer with consistent branding and links
- **OptimizedImage Component**: Custom image component with responsive srcset generation and lazy loading
- **BaseLayout Component**: Template wrapper for consistent page structure, metadata, and scripts

### 2.2 Component Features
- Active page state management for navigation highlighting
- Mobile-responsive navigation menu
- TypeScript type safety for all components
- Preserved Webflow animation data attributes for compatibility

---

## 3. Form Processing & Backend Integration

### 3.1 API Endpoint Development
- Created 3 server-side API endpoints for form processing:
  - `/api/contact` - Contact form handler
  - `/api/join-team` - Job application form handler
  - `/api/acquisitions` - Business acquisition inquiry handler

### 3.2 Form Security & Validation
- Integrated Cloudflare Turnstile bot protection on all forms
- Implemented server-side form validation
- Added email format validation and sanitization
- Created client-side form submission handlers with error handling

### 3.3 Email Service Integration
- Integrated Resend email service for form submissions
- Created HTML email templates for professional form notifications
- Configured reply-to functionality for direct response capability
- Set up environment variable configuration for email credentials

---

## 4. Image Optimization & Performance

### 4.1 Image Optimization System
- Implemented responsive image component with automatic srcset generation
- Created support for multiple image widths (500px, 800px, 1080px, 1600px, 2000px, 2600px)
- Added lazy loading for improved page performance
- Configured Astro image optimization settings

### 4.2 Image Migration
- Migrated all project images to optimized local storage
- Organized images in structured directory system
- Maintained image quality while optimizing file sizes

---

## 5. Animation & Interaction System

### 5.1 Custom Animation Implementation
- Created vanilla JavaScript animation system to replace Webflow's proprietary animations
- Implemented scroll-triggered animations using Intersection Observer API
- Recreated fade-in animations for page elements
- Preserved all original animation timing and effects

### 5.2 Interactive Components
- Replaced Webflow tabs component with custom vanilla JS implementation
- Recreated mobile navigation menu functionality
- Maintained all original user interaction patterns

---

## 6. Development Environment & Configuration

### 6.1 Project Setup
- Configured Astro project with TypeScript support
- Set up Node.js adapter for server-side rendering
- Created hybrid rendering mode (static + API routes)
- Configured development and production build processes

### 6.2 TypeScript Integration
- Added TypeScript configuration and type checking
- Created type definitions for all components and utilities
- Implemented type safety across the entire codebase

### 6.3 Utility Libraries
- Created `animations.ts` - Animation and interaction utilities
- Created `email.ts` - Email sending functionality
- Created `form-handler.ts` - Client-side form processing
- Created `turnstile.ts` - Cloudflare Turnstile verification utility

---

## 7. Deployment & Infrastructure

### 7.1 Railway Deployment Configuration
- Configured Railway deployment settings (railway.json)
- Set up Node.js server configuration for production
- Created Procfile for process management
- Configured environment variable structure

### 7.2 Server Configuration
- Set up Express server for static file serving (legacy support)
- Configured Astro SSR adapter for server-side rendering
- Set up port and host configuration for Railway hosting

### 7.3 Environment Variables Setup
- Configured Turnstile site and secret keys
- Set up Resend API key for email service
- Created email configuration (from/to addresses)
- Documented all required environment variables

---

## 8. Additional Features

### 8.1 Documentation
- Created comprehensive conversion guide (CONVERSION_GUIDE.md)
- Wrote form setup documentation (FORM_SETUP.md)
- Created Turnstile setup guide (TURNSTILE_SETUP.md)
- Documented Railway deployment process (RAILWAY_DEPLOYMENT.md)
- Created Astro setup documentation (ASTRO_SETUP.md)
- Provided migration analysis and project README

---

## 9. Testing & Quality Assurance

### 9.1 Cross-Page Testing
- Verified all internal links and navigation
- Tested responsive design across all pages
- Validated form submissions and error handling
- Confirmed asset loading and path resolution

### 9.2 Browser Compatibility
- Maintained compatibility with all modern browsers
- Preserved Webflow CSS classes for styling consistency
- Ensured JavaScript functionality across browsers

---

## 10. Code Organization & Best Practices

### 10.1 Project Structure
- Organized codebase with clear separation of concerns
- Created logical directory structure (components, layouts, pages, lib)
- Maintained clean, readable, and maintainable code

### 10.2 Asset Management
- Organized all static assets in public directory
- Maintained original asset structure for compatibility
- Created optimized image storage system

---

## Technical Specifications

- **Framework**: Astro JS 5.0
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Deployment Platform**: Railway
- **Email Service**: Resend
- **Bot Protection**: Cloudflare Turnstile
- **Image Optimization**: Astro Image Component
- **Server**: Node.js with Express
- **Total Pages Converted**: 9 pages
- **API Endpoints Created**: 3 endpoints
- **Components Created**: 4 reusable components
- **Utility Libraries**: 4 TypeScript modules

---

## Deliverables Summary

✅ Complete website migration from Webflow to Astro JS  
✅ All 9 pages converted and functional  
✅ Form processing system with 3 API endpoints  
✅ Email notification system integrated  
✅ Bot protection (Cloudflare Turnstile) implemented  
✅ Image optimization system  
✅ Custom animation system  
✅ Mobile-responsive navigation  
✅ Railway deployment configuration  
✅ Comprehensive documentation  
✅ TypeScript type safety throughout  

---

## Notes

- All original Webflow styling and classes preserved for design consistency
- Site maintains 100% visual and functional parity with original Webflow site
- Enhanced with modern development practices and improved performance
- Ready for production deployment on Railway platform

HOME PAGE:

Paul Massad Landscaping name and logo made larger; 

Logo is now 20% larger 

Photos on home page centered rather than left justified;

Done. You are not utilizing the linking that I added to the homepage tiles. I have mentioned this several times. http://drops.eliteweblabs.com/1A3I393G2q06

Icons for facebook, email, phone, etc. made smaller and moved to bottom of page

Done
 

DESIGN PROCESS PAGE: 

amenities is spelled wrong

Same issue as the Xmas tree graphic... I do not write or manage content. The typo was made by whoever wrote the content.

BLOG PAGE:

When you click on this, the heading of the page says "Projects" not "Blogs".  We would like it to say "Blogs"

Done as Blog. 'Blogs' implies that there is more than one 'blog' which is not proper in this instance.  

 

CONTACT PAGE:

inconsistent fonts

Done http://drops.eliteweblabs.com/3I1W0V1Y1V3e
 

PRODUCTS:

Brittanie has been unable to add items to this page.  It autofills amounts and doesn't work right;

Fixed http://drops.eliteweblabs.com/332c03263c3l

Remove sentence under product heading

Done http://drops.eliteweblabs.com/0X2O0u3B1a0m
 

OVERALL: 

We want to be able to edit every section of every page -   from headers to pictures to body content: right now there is only the capability to edit roughly ½ of these thing on any given page. 

This is not true whatsoever. 














 

When the above items are complete and we pay you in full, we would like the following:

 

1.We want to get the login username and password for your domain registrar.  We want complete control over this account so we can do what we want with it. I believe our domain is paulmassadlanscaping.com. 

I can send you the EPP / transfer code to move your domain

2. We want to get the username/password for the current website builder (I believe it's wordpress) that you are using to build the website to allow work to be done.  We want the url to the wordpress login and username password to get into the wordpress site.

You have had this info since day one. 
login = http://paulmassadlandscaping.com/login
username = PaulMassad
password = reset it on the login page if you do not have it

3. We want you to hand over the keys to the domain registration, hosting, and wordpress login and account.

duly noted

FYI this additional work took me 3.75 hours. Please
send transfer credentials

line_items.description: 04/10/2018 - Localhost Setup, WordPress Install, Stripe Account, Square, Kiosk Logistics Research
04/11/2018 - Shipping plugin research

04/12/2018 - QR Code automatically generated for the product in wordpress admin.




​I took a picture of the QR code above, reverse engineered a web based scanner and presto it scanned and provided the url.. all I need to do from there is redirect to the product.



and while in the process of developing this I learned that if you open your camera app (as of iOS 11 / June 2017, and it looks like certain Android phones had the same capabilities over a year before) and aim it at a QR it prompts you to load the webpage..

normally it would say 'Paradigm Hydroseed' instead of 'Notification' but I took this screenshot while my phone was locked / private.



​
 easy as that!

​I think that given the last discovery, I wouldn't even bother with advertising / using a separate domain like I was thinking at lunch. Reason being is that I can prefix the URL in each QR code to automatically load the 'onsite' version of paradigmhydroseed.com. Once the visitor is in an 'onsite session' I can then include the web based scanner I developed earlier so that the visitor does not have to leave the website and open the camera again.. totally streamlined.

Onsite I would go with a placard with the product name, price, description, instructions (open camera, point camera), etc and include something like this to indicate.





​Thats gotta cover your ass for ​at least ​95% of people that have the ability to drive onto your yard​ and have a phone.
04/13/2018 - Build out site
04/14/2018 - Product +1 / -1 toggle / woocommerce cart
04/15/2018 - QR Plugin build / template styles
04/16/2018 - Usability
04/21/2018 - Template / mobile styles
04/22/2018 - Usability tweaking
04/24/2018 - Checkout style
04/25/2018 - Checkout / delivery / delivery invoices
04/26/2018 - Shipping
04/26/2018 - Publish site, test on live domain, full fail on webqr.com js kit, research, determine https://cozmo.github.io/jsQR/ works on mobile and is pro

11/24/2017 - hey Pete, 

I'm doing some server maintenance and figured I'd check your site traffic and was shocked. 

http://drops.eliteweblabs.com/421T2A1J1B0v

Literally 5 visits in 60 days. I've had clients complain about not getting results online while getting over 100 hits a day.

I thought that maybe I had forgotten to release the site to search engines but not the case. I've never seen anything like this for a site that has been online this long and everything checks out (mostly) when I do a google search http://drops.eliteweblabs.com/0a1z2l3m3m20

I did a check at traffic experts moz.com and there were no issues that would result in this lack of traffic. http://drops.eliteweblabs.com/0W1k340A1p1W. A 5% rating I would understand, but 58% is not something that would cause this.

I'm supremely baffled here. Granted there is not a lot of page content on your site but google doesn't care about that like it used to. 

My only guess is that there is something about the code in the theme you picked out that is inhibiting traffic.

There's a few things we can do that won't take long to see if they have any effect before any drastic measures are taken.

Let me know..

t
Discount

Apply / Squares - 07/21/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: Hi Jessica, I work with Kelvin Paulino and Sonia's Auto Sales in Worcester MA.

We would like to implement another iteration of the form from Dealer Track on http://www.soniasautosales.com/web/credit_application on another domain for their corresponding finance company's website. 

Hence forth an additional form is needed to track the data, can you provide me with the best protocol?

Also could you please let me know what the styling options are for the form? 

Additionally, Is there a way to simplify the form or another form that can be used with less fields?

and Finally, is there any API access where an HTML form could be submit to your database?

Thank you,

Tom Senecal
sen@eliteweblabs.com

I just left him a detailed voicemail as he did not answer.
Apply / Squares - 08/11/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: I just talked to Levi to explain again he said he needs to talk to the web team and then hed call back

Apply / Squares - 08/11/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: they just called me back and said that because allautofinancial.com isn't a dealer track website they can't (won't is my guess) do it..

they saying that they have no way to track it. 

if you want I can put a 'heatmap' on the site. it shows where people click so you can at least tell which one is most popular

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Flywheel Support <help@getflywheel.com>
Date: Wed, Sep 9, 2015 at 12:37 PM
Subject: Re: Site is showing as hacked
To: sen@eliteweblabs.com


Hey Thomas,

Thanks for letting us know about your malware concerns for this site. We’re running some scans right now to see if we can spot any infected files. This can take some time, since it’s something that we do in several phases. Apologies for that in advance. We’ll be sure to let you know when everything looks clear on our end.

While we’re working on that, one thing that can be done on your end is updating all themes and plugins to their most recent version, uninstalling any that aren’t being used, as well as changing passwords on any user accounts to something that is as secure as possible. Since old plugin/theme versions and weak passwords are the primary ways that WordPress sites become infected with malware, taking care of this as soon as is possible will also help us to make sure that reinfection doesn’t happen as we clean. Let us know once that has been done, and we’ll run some final scans on the site to make sure everything was caught.

Let us know if you have any questions. We’re happy to answer them.

Thanks,
Aaron

line_items.description:
BDB Web Design - 09/27/2016 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: 

'we' don't have a woocom account, I do, and thats how I have gotten all the plugins I use but an account is free so feel free to join if you need something, but I don't recommend installing / configuring any plugins on the live site, at least not without talking to me first about it..

the woocommerce store front is mostly already integrated, that was what the template switch was all about for the shirts, however, bc we're just using the paypal generated buttons we're only using about 25% woocom, bypassing add-to-cart, checkout, etc. and you don't need any SQL knowledge to use the woocom admin whatsoever. or wordpress.

as for the email account, are you trying to integrate into a standard @gmail account or a google apps? I ask bc I think theres a difference.. but you wanna look for this 


Screen Shot 2016-09-27 at 9.24.14 PM.png

send and check email from another account is what you want then it should just be a matter of enter server and login/pw but if you get stuck just yell/email 'I suck at Google' and I'll get right on that 

also I finalllly got HotJar.com setup after 3 back and forths with support, its world class heatmapping / analytics you should have both gotten an invite. it just started collecting data so don't wig out on anything just yet.. flywheel stats say we're averaging 5000 hits per day, with an 8k every now and then.. pretty amazing and about 4 times more than anyone else I host.

BDB Web Design - 09/27/2016 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: woooa eassy there cowboy!!


Bypassing the add to cart, checkout is awesome for paid traffic but I'm wanting to have an entire store available that's sortable for different products. We're going to be using drops shipping fulfillment centers so once we start scaling we'll be able to grow the  entire store fairly quickly. 

only doing this bc AFAIK, this has been the plan so far but yeah we can go full-wootardcommerce

The plugins I was looking at are 

Way better picture display
http://codecanyon.net/item/woothumbs-awesome-product-imagery/ 

I have this, from woocommerce / woothemes, which is pretty fly

down the road theres branding the email templates, product returns, coupon codes, etc to consider if pertinent. 

the layout/theme is selected in wordpress, the files in the theme generate the site framework by rendering html via php, css style sheets make it pretty, all running on a linux server, connected to a mysql database and that would be what you call a "WordPress LAMP (linux/apache/mysql/php) Stack".

so to change the layout, the php templates and css would be the place.. but.. if you want to try that yourself, I can set you up with a testing server.. if you don't have much experience I recommend checking out treehouse.com or envato tutorials / classes to start as css/html/php not super 'jump into-able' without a background.. im at about 15 years of self learning my damn self..

line_items.description:
Updates Server security patches, zero-day Wordpress updates, PHP/MySQL updates and more. Your server and sites will always be running the latest software, ensuring speed and security.

Backup Daily database backups and weekly backups of every file on the server. You will never lose content even in the event of catastrophic server failure or user error.

Uptime Monitoring Your sites are constantly monitored (every 5 minutes) and in the rare case of downtime, we begin addressing the problem immediately, free of charge, unless it is determined to be user error.

Stream is the easiest and safest way to track content changes happening to your WordPress site and then view them in beautifully organized detail. Users make mistakes, Stream can help you save hours later.

CDN Content Delivery Network: The website content is distributed to 23 data centers across the world, automatically caching static files edge nodes so they are geographically closer to visitors. Sites load on average twice as fast, reducing hops and lowering latency. This also increases page rank in search engines as speed is a factor.

Broken Link / 404 Reporting - Search engines save & check URLs from the day they are posted, users bookmark pages and come back years later and they don't exist...Whenever a user or search engine lands on a page that doesn't exist, it is logged and at the end of every month all broken links are setup as .htaccess redirects so they land at their proper locations. This will increase search rank by decreasing dead end inbound links.

Broken Link Checker - If a user posts a sloppy comment, an editor makes a goof or a link simply drops off, not a problem. BLC checks your content site wide for broken links, marks them with strikethrough text on the front end and reports them to the Admin. At the end of every month all broken links will be addressed, in the name of Search Engine Optimization!

Google™ Analytics Dashboard Widget - See site performance, time on site, bounce rate and more right from the WordPress™ Dashboard.

HTML Validation - HTML errors in code hurt page rank and slow page load. At the end of every month the entire website will be scanned for errors against the current standards. Although rare, any errors that are reported back that are generated by WordPress™ or a content edit are fixed free of charge 99% of the time. However if an assigned user pastes content that produces errors, Elite Web Labs, llc. is not responsible to repair but will likely fix up to a few of them regardless.

Broken Link / 404 Reporting - Search engines save & check URLs from the day they are posted, users bookmark pages and come back years later and they don't exist...Whenever a user or search engine lands on a page that doesn't exist, it is logged and at the end of every month all broken links are setup as .htaccess redirects so they land at their proper locations. This will increase search rank by decreasing dead end inbound links.

Broken Link Checker - If a user posts a sloppy comment, an editor makes a goof or a link simply drops off, not a problem. BLC checks your content site wide for broken links, marks them with strikethrough text on the front end and reports them to the Admin. At the end of every month all broken links will be addressed, in the name of Search Engine Optimization!

Google™ Analytics Dashboard Widget - See site performance, time on site, bounce rate and more right from the WordPress™ Dashboard.

HTML Validation - HTML errors in code hurt page rank and slow page load. At the end of every month the entire website will be scanned for errors against the current standards. Although rare, any errors that are reported back that are generated by WordPress™ or a content edit are fixed free of charge 99% of the time. However if an assigned user pastes content that produces errors, Elite Web Labs, llc. is not responsible to repair but will likely fix up to a few of them regardless.

Post Launch - Web Design Hourly - 10/23/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: OUTSIDE SCOPE - UPDATE BOXIT DUE TO SECURITY ISSUE


hi Renato, I took care of this update as well as all other plugins, only took about 15 minutes.

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: The Envato Team 
Date: Mon, Oct 19, 2015 at 9:48 AM
Subject: Envato Market Security Notice: Boxit
To: eliteweblabs 


 
Envato Account
Hello eliteweblabs,

We are getting in touch to let you know about a security vulnerability in the BOXIT - The Dropbox file upload for Wordpress plugin versions prior to 2.7 (releases prior to October 19, 2015).

This is a severe vulnerability and you should take action now.

We have been working with Sommersethdesign, the creators of Boxit, who have addressed this vulnerability.

What You Should Do

In order to secure your website from this vulnerability, we strongly encourage you to update to version 2.7 or later as soon as possible.

You can update Boxit through the Plugins screen in WordPress. It should automatically detect the new version and show a prompt similar to the one in this screenshot: http://envato.d.pr/bMwW/4Pl5gUyp. Click "update now" to update.

If Boxit is not activated, you will not see the prompt to update to the new version. However, you are still vulnerable. Please either delete Boxit from your server or activate it and update to 2.7.

If Boxit is activated but for some reason the update prompt does not appear, you will need to download the item manually from CodeCanyon and upload it to your server. Log into CodeCanyon and go your Downloads, locate Boxit and download it. Unzip the file and FTP the boxit folder to wp-content/plugins/, overwriting the existing files. 

You should double check whether you have updated successfully by checking the version number for Boxit on the Plugins screen in WordPress. Please make sure that the version number says 2.7 or higher.

If you have used this plugin in projects for clients, please help them to secure their sites as well.

Your

line_items.description:
Updates Server security patches, zero-day Wordpress updates, PHP/MySQL updates and more. Your server and sites will always be running the latest software, ensuring speed and security.

Backup Daily database backups and weekly backups of every file on the server. You will never lose content even in the event of catastrophic server failure or user error.

Uptime Monitoring Your sites are constantly monitored (every 5 minutes) and in the rare case of downtime, we begin addressing the problem immediately, free of charge, unless it is determined to be user error.

Stream is the easiest and safest way to track content changes happening to your WordPress site and then view them in beautifully organized detail. Users make mistakes, Stream can help you save hours later.

CDN Content Delivery Network: The website content is distributed to 23 data centers across the world, automatically caching static files edge nodes so they are geographically closer to visitors. Sites load on average twice as fast, reducing hops and lowering latency. This also increases page rank in search engines as speed is a factor.

Broken Link / 404 Reporting - Search engines save & check URLs from the day they are posted, users bookmark pages and come back years later and they don't exist...Whenever a user or search engine lands on a page that doesn't exist, it is logged and at the end of every month all broken links are setup as .htaccess redirects so they land at their proper locations. This will increase search rank by decreasing dead end inbound links.

Broken Link Checker - If a user posts a sloppy comment, an editor makes a goof or a link simply drops off, not a problem. BLC checks your content site wide for broken links, marks them with strikethrough text on the front end and reports them to the Admin. At the end of every month all broken links will be addressed, in the name of Search Engine Optimization!

Google™ Analytics Dashboard Widget - See site performance, time on site, bounce rate and more right from the WordPress™ Dashboard.

HTML Validation - HTML errors in code hurt page rank and slow page load. At the end of every month the entire website will be scanned for errors against the current standards. Although rare, any errors that are reported back that are generated by WordPress™ or a content edit are fixed free of charge 99% of the time. However if an assigned user pastes content that produces errors, Elite Web Labs, llc. is not responsible to repair but will likely fix up to a few of them regardless.

SFH WordPress - 10/31/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: Hi V,

Im not sure how Muse works, I've found that the best practice is to avoid anything that gets between you and the website source code as it teaches you to learn the app instead of the code.

I wondered... is it possible to do this with Wordpress

theres a couple ways to tackle this, they're not wordpress specific. CSS manages the way the elements on a website look and CSS supports screen resolution queries so you can check if a visitor is on a certain size screen and adjust the site accordingly. You can also setup a mobile specific site that delivers different content to mobile viewers, usually simplified..

as for developing an app, a true 'app' needs to be submit to review to apple / android and is not just a mobile version of your website. It requires download and it should have additional functionality. users shouldn't have to download an app to view an adjusted version of your website, it can be done properly in a browser.

hope this helps,

line_items.description:
Updates Server security patches, zero-day Wordpress updates, PHP/MySQL updates and more. Your server and sites will always be running the latest software, ensuring speed and security.

Backup Daily database backups and weekly backups of every file on the server. You will never lose content even in the event of catastrophic server failure or user error.

Uptime Monitoring Your sites are constantly monitored (every 5 minutes) and in the rare case of downtime, we begin addressing the problem immediately, free of charge, unless it is determined to be user error.

Stream is the easiest and safest way to track content changes happening to your WordPress site and then view them in beautifully organized detail. Users make mistakes, Stream can help you save hours later.

CDN Content Delivery Network: The website content is distributed to 23 data centers across the world, automatically caching static files edge nodes so they are geographically closer to visitors. Sites load on average twice as fast, reducing hops and lowering latency. This also increases page rank in search engines as speed is a factor.

Broken Link / 404 Reporting - Search engines save & check URLs from the day they are posted, users bookmark pages and come back years later and they don't exist...Whenever a user or search engine lands on a page that doesn't exist, it is logged and at the end of every month all broken links are setup as .htaccess redirects so they land at their proper locations. This will increase search rank by decreasing dead end inbound links.

Broken Link Checker - If a user posts a sloppy comment, an editor makes a goof or a link simply drops off, not a problem. BLC checks your content site wide for broken links, marks them with strikethrough text on the front end and reports them to the Admin. At the end of every month all broken links will be addressed, in the name of Search Engine Optimization!

Google™ Analytics Dashboard Widget - See site performance, time on site, bounce rate and more right from the WordPress™ Dashboard.

HTML Validation - HTML errors in code hurt page rank and slow page load. At the end of every month the entire website will be scanned for errors against the current standards. Although rare, any errors that are reported back that are generated by WordPress™ or a content edit are fixed free of charge 99% of the time. However if an assigned user pastes content that produces errors, Elite Web Labs, llc. is not responsible to repair but will likely fix up to a few of them regardless.

PS.  I have a question regarding pictures on a website.  Someone has asked me to take pics for their website.  Is there a standard size that the .jpg’s should be saved as?
 
From: Thomas Senecal [mailto:sen@eliteweblabs.com] 
Sent: Tuesday, December 01, 2015 8:50 AM


Thomas Senecal 
2:21 PM (1 minute ago)

to Janet 
ok, ill get this finished up in the next couple days,

for website pics, generally you don't need to get too crazy.. web resolution is pretty low compared to print.

for a full screen image (like a background, or the CARE homepage) I generally stick to 1600px wide at 72dpi (web resolution) and I down sample to 30-40% in photoshop which drastically reduces file size without sacrificing too much quality (jpg only).

If you're doing something like employee headshots, I would do about 600px wide. with retina displays and touch device zoom you want content images to be about twice the size they display.

good luck!

From: CSSJockey Helpdesk <helpdesk@cssjockey.com>
Date: Mon, Aug 3, 2015 at 3:51 AM
Subject: [SRTKBPY60C1570] New comment from Mohit Aneja on auto login
To: sen@eliteweblabs.com


eliteweblabs,

Mohit Aneja added a new comment to auto login.

Hi,

Sorry for the delay in reply, I was out of town due to some family emergency with no access to internet.

I checked your site setup and I see some errors which result the AJAX redirection to fail. You can try following steps to avoid this issue.

1. Disable wp_debug in wp-config.php so the correct Url can be returned by the AJAX call.

2. Disable AJAX on login and registration forms so normal form submit can work and redirect the user after registration and login actions. 

Please feel free to get back to me for more information or help on this.

SAE frontend art build - 07/22/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: investigate SF options

it seems like some of this may be accomplished with https://zapier.com and https://wordpress.org/plugins/hookpress/ but might be beyond my pay grade.
We use the 'artwork' custom post type to manage / sort via the post author (our 'customer' author role) and all posts will need to be imported as such author role as publisher of all corresponding 'artwork' posts. (this is just FYI not super concerning for adding art manually)
to create users in SF, WP author roles need to be checked, press / customer (exhibitor) / vip 
the logic to create an opportunity in SF is tricky since not all accounts are going to be new they need to trigger somehow and they need to ID by which show they are registering for. I think the way to do it is set a hidden category value for the artwork post frontend upload but it would need to be set for each show, without some automation. Then we can create an opportunity in SF based on each time an author's post is published to the new show category, but only needs to create one opportunity / Zap once... but this means we need to force artwork upload or they could skip it unless the skip button can also be config'd to automatically publish a dummy post to trigger the Zap
each artist name field per exhibitor needs to sent to SF as CSV
this is ninja territory.. and I have plateful as it is.

I kinda feel like this is overkill in the syncing department, we're basically mirroring services here.. for what?? just my $.02

I gotta get to the wireframes but obviously we'll be discussing this further / with your guy..

SAE frontend art build - 07/22/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: Year was not in CT's dataset

I added it right above 'edition' I figured that was fitting?

for WordPress™ SEO module, you can go to 'screen options' at top right and uncheck it so it hides from backend editor page.

frontend submissions are automatically set to draft

if an artwork is used in multiple shows, you can certainly select multiple categories to display it. 
BUT this is only in the backend admin, so frontend submission will not have that ability as it is only meant to work for the current show, this importing is only a temporary measure to bring in the old content and is not designed to be a full featured submission (we're not gonna ask them what show this artwork is for, once live), thats why I asked to be notified after an entire show was done, so I can go in and set them all to the appropriate show.

determining if an artwork post has already been uploaded takes a little human element.. basically the only way to tell 100% is to check the artwork CPT for the author/exhibitor however IF an artwork is created twice with with the same title, wordpress will add '-2' or '-3' etc to the post permalink by default and they can be cleaned up by that logic afterwards.

WordPress Consult - 09/08/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: hi All, heres my thoughts on your points as well as some other stuff I noticed..

*         Adjust picture size on home page.  For example if you click on "keeping it clean" the three blog posts do not line up, do we need to use a certain dimension picture size or is this a coding thing?

this is called 'cascading'. there are a couple options, you can force the height so they grid up but your theme uses mansory.js which doesn't seem to be working properly. Masonry javascript calculates the height of the tiles so they all fit nice (Pinterest) could be from the errors (mentioned below)

*         Picture sizes on the slider are not the same

this can be fixed by forcing the height or using images with the same aspect ratio (width X height needs to be the ratio).

*         Remove "about me" under each blog post - you will see when you open the actual post (on bottom of page)

I can hide 'about me' but then theres a big blank space..  unless I hide the avatar part too.. I think you'd be better off to use / fix this. FUWF2015 = Sarah? if thats the case then I suggest for continuity that your WordPress™ author names are the same.
Inline image 3

*         Figure out how to shut down Weebly website, and activate Wordpress

Not sure on this, seems to be right? http://www.fedupwithfake.com/ is connecting to your WordPress™ site, is there another URL?

*         Set up emails @fedupwithfake (for each of us - Wendy, Sarah, Carolyn).  We have been going back and forth with google because did not have log in, they had to verify we owned site, yada, yada...we are waiting on response from them hopefully today.

I suggest google apps for business https://www.google.com/work/apps/business/ for your email, calendar, etc, I've been using it for years, as well as for dozens of clients. It's $5 per user per month or you can defer to whatever email your hosting company provides. Don't worry about trying to contact Google™, I can reset your account via your domain if your password is los

WordPress Consult - 10/08/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: so this is kinda lame.. your theme was pulled from the envato marketplace.. it happens once in a while, usually because of poor support but theres nothing you can do about it. 

Inline image 1


mailing list pop-over is working, I set it to auto-load after 5 seconds. I left it on the footer as well as its a best practice to have it available still.

WordPress Consult - 10/08/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: it just means that you're stuck with the theme copy that you have...

a couple years ago a vulnerability was found in a commonly used script that affected millions of wordpress websites. if something like that happens again, involving your theme, the only way to fix it is via independent support, the original developer will not be releasing an update as it is no longer in the marketplace.

if it helps you feel better, I have about $3000 worth of themes, scripts, etc that are of the same status..

you can always access webmail here is tutorial 

https://kb.site5.com/email/webmail-overview/#sa

I set it to 400px tall. you still need to use bigger images. width = 800px minimum max 1600px

ACP Web Design - 06/29/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: From: Grimes, Steve <steve.grimes@thomsonreuters.com>
Date: Mon, Jun 29, 2015 at 11:55 AM
Subject: Aitken, Chacharone & Power – Website Launch Technical PreWork - 2nd Request
To: seth@acplawoffices.com, "Flynn, Casey" <casey.flynn@thomsonreuters.com>


Hello Seth,

After Casey gets your website built and you have approved it, I'll be working to get your new site live using the domains, worcester-legal.com, chacharonepower.com, aitkenlawoffice.com and acplawoffices.com

To get your site live, we'll need to have your domains repointed at your hosting company for each and I'll also need to know if anyone at the office is using email on any of the domains.

For example, I can see that you are using an email address on @acplawoffices.com. Does anyone in your office use email that ends with @worcester-legal.com, @chacharonepower.com or @aitkenlawoffice.com?

For the domains worcester-legal.com and chacharonepower.com, both are registered to Mr. Chacharone through Clickandname.com (phone: 800-480-3369). If you can provide me the login credentials for each of the domains at http://clickandname.com/ , that will allow me to repoint these domains for you when the site is ready to launch.

For the domains aitkenlawoffice.com and acplawoffices.com, both of these domains appear to be registered through Inmotionhosting.com (phone: 888-321-4678 x2). If you have the login credentials for your control panel for each of the domains at Inmotionhosting, I can repoint them for you when the site is ready to launch.

The email hosting on acplawoffices.com is through Google. Can you confirm that you have administrative access to your Google email through your Google apps account?  If so, that will verify I can keep the email running on the domain when the domain is repointed through Inmotionhosting.

Alternately, if you have a tech person or tech company who handles your domain/website issues, please feel free to forward this email to them and you can copy me on that forward which will allo

ACP Web Design - 08/21/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: that is fine with me, I suggested worcester-legal as it was strong for keywords but there clearly hasn't been much link building done.

please advise.

thx
Tom

ACP Web Design - 08/24/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: this is all set, 

I also pointed additional domain from way back chacharonestiles.com to 167.68.37.150 as well as worcester-legal.com

I do not think any of these are valid emails

Inline image 1

I cannot find a way to add MX records at inmotion but I dont think it matters unless the above emails are needed.

Inline image 2

Hello Seth,

Your Project Manager Casey has informed me that you have approved the new site and we're ready to launch it using the domains, aitkenlawoffice.com, chacharonepower.com and acplawoffices.com.

To do so, we'll need your IT person, Thomas Senecal, from Elite Web Labs, who I've copied on this message, to change the "A" record IP and CNAME address for the following:

Please point www.aitkenlawoffice.com to the following CNAME ->  fs3.cdn.findlaw.com

And please change the 'A' record IP address on aitkenlawoffice.com to: 167.68.37.150

Note: this domain's MX record at Inmotionhosting is the domain name itself. If anyone uses email that ends with @aitkenlawoffice.com, that MX record will need to be modified; From my experience with Inmotionhosting, the simplest way to do that is to update the existing CNAME record for "mail" to an 'A' record pointing to the domain's existing IP address on the root domain (98.129.229.222) and then change the MX record to mail.aitkenlawoffice.com. In that way, the domain's MX record will continue to point to its email server.

The updates for the other two domains, chacharonepower.com and acplawoffices.com, that are with Rackspace will not require the same modifications because both domains use Google's MX records.

For chacharonepower.com and acplawoffices.com, please change the "A" record IP address one each of the following to: 167.68.37.150

chacharonepower.com
www.chacharonepower.com
acplawoffices.com
www.acplawoffices.com

Once the updates have been completed, please contact me so I can verify the change.

If you or Thomas have any questions or need further assistance, please feel free to contact me.

ACP Web Design - 09/14/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: Andre B: This domain has been suspended by ICANN because its ownership verification was not completed. Is mdc@chacharonestiles.com a valid email address?
You: ehh not sure
You: how did you determine?
You: if you dont mind
Andre B: Because the domain was registered through the Cloud Sites control panel, we're able to view the status of the domain and its history. On 30 August 2015 the domain was ultimately suspended, with ICANN's verification emails having been sent on August 30, 27, 25, 19, 14 and 13th.
You: that happens even though the domain was registered for years?
Andre B: Maintaining valid contact information on a domain name is part of what ICANN mandates for all registrations. While the generally test this information immediately following registration, they are also known to "spot check" domains that have been registered for some time, which is why ensuring that the contact information for the domain's primary contact is up to date and valid.

ACP Web Design - 09/15/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: Hello Thomas,
 
Because of the previous issues with the site on that domain, Google had flagged the domain as a potential risk for users who searched for it.
 
For that reason, it was removed as a redirect to the new site because the status with Google would likely affect the new site’s performance. It would be best to remove the domain’s DNS so it will be a dead URL.
 
Thank you,
Steve Grimes
Client Technical Services

FBG - Social Media Management - 07/29/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: Tom,

My name is Eric Ogren and I am part of CircleUp’s Business Development team. I am reaching out to you with respect to our interest in Mick Morgan’s. At CircleUp, we look to connect small consumer brands with investors who have significant experience and a passion for the consumer space. Since our founding in April of 2012, we have partnered with and helped over 90 entrepreneurs raise $100M in growth equity. Through our platform, we seek to help companies raise more quickly and efficiently than what is afforded in the offline world. More importantly, our sole focus in on the consumer market, so many of our investors come with a deep knowledge of the industry and are able to provide guidance and support for companies. This strategic support can be invaluable to some in attaining their growth objectives.

The reason I reached out to Mick Morgan’s is that we are looking for innovative brands such as yours to invest in and partner with to aid in growth efforts. We not only look to bring value in the fundraise effort, we also look to provide strategic partnerships. Some of our select strategic partners include General Mills, Procter & Gamble, Johnson & Johnson, Virgin America and SPINS. These partners provide entrepreneurs the opportunity to learn from some of the most successful brands in the world.

I look forward to sharing how we may be of service in helping to not only build a strong brand, but also provide the necessary capital to take the business to the next level. It would be very helpful if you could start an initial profile on our site at https://circleup.com/raise-funds to share basic information about Mick Morgan’s. This will help make our initial discussion more productive and should only take a few minutes.

Regards,

Eric


Eric S. Ogren
Business Development Executive
CircleUp.com
eogren@circleup.com
 
Know a great consumer product or retail brand that is interested in raising funds on CircleUp?
https://circleup.com/raise-funds/ 

Unsubscribe from Co

FBG - Social Media Management - 08/11/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: its depends on whats going on. theres a couple things here and it doesn't seem quite right.

the domain's DNS is pointed to wordpress.com (wordpress.org and wordpress.com are very different, .org you own everything, .com they own everything) which is not in use anywhere as far as I can tell. So instead of your domain pointing right at the website, its going to wordpress.com which is then telling it to go to Weebly.

the site at http://69.42.84.209/~fedupwit/ is hosted at http://www.site5.com/ and thats where you would likely need to create email accounts however I recommend using https://www.google.com/work/apps/business/ if you don't mind paying 5 dollars a month per user.

MHS Web Design - 01/21/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: Hey Jan, That totally makes sense and I understand nobody ever wants to see a bill for more than double the estimate.

Call it 119 hours for the sake of simplicity. I use https://www.getharvest.com/ which tracks everything I do (to the minute) and even knows if I step away for a phone call etc.

At my target rate of $75/hr (which is low for the industry) 119 hours is $8925.

$7,138.80 is what I requested after 20% off which brings me under $60/hr.

Factor in taxes and anything less than $50/hr is not acceptable for this level of web development and honestly I don't think many small / independent businesses can even operate at that margin. I can't write this off and I'm already shorting myself almost 3k to a client I have an extensive history with yet never had a single glitch or hiccup with anything. I was delegated to manage all the updates to the MHS sites because every company that was allowed to access the websites were messing them up. 

I took the 20% off because I went the extra mile (not sure how that makes sense in the end but I did) and went backwards to give CARE exactly what they wanted / needed. This was one of several factors, and I think 20% covers it. A lesser developer would have just left it as it was but thats not how I operate.

If the overages did not get communicated to accounting or CARE or whomever that isn't my fault but is anyone at this level honestly not familiar with the fact that when a project goes long it parallels the budget?

If $6500 (for the entire CARE site and rest of mobile testing) total resolves this so we can continue on I can live with that.

MHS Web Design - 07/01/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: I can safely assure you, that between the hosting company (I looked into it, the sites are being hosting thru another company called http://asmallorange.com/ ) and the site design (page size and file requests are waaay too high, see below) MHS is effectively shooting themselves in the foot in search engines and user experience. 


test yourself here http://tools.pingdom.com/fpt/#!/eLe97Y/http://overlook-mass.org/


In this day you simply cannot expect visitors to wait.. user engagement and page load time are basically married metrics... and Google also takes page load time into account for rankings.


This is my baby, we're a top-billing global art show in a fiercely competitive market with less than a dozen other companies in the World doing what we do. I have optimized this website and it runs on my Flywheel server blazingly fast and thats where your stats need to be if you expect to compete. 



in closing..

-4mb is an ETERNITY on a mobile device without wifi.

-WordPress™ has been and will continue to be the most popular CMS in the World, it is always going to be the subject of constant attacks. Even with employing all best practices, consistent updating of plugins / Wordpress, you're still vulnerable. The timthumb exploit affected millions of wordpress site's themes, which many developers stopped or simply didn't care to update / fix leaving many sites crippled.

- Be sure to get managed WordPress™ hosting, with managed security, malware/attack repair, managed WP update installation (timely), OR be sure that you will never be billed for a fix due to an attack as well as a timeframe for repairs.

- This logic / hosting offering has only been around for the last year or so and there's only a handful of them. 

wpengine.com is the biggest but I did not like them and cancelled
pagely.com
getflywheel.com  so far so good
pressable.com

EBS-Wordpress - 06/04/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: setup google cal / run test orders
 
Step 1: Google Developers Console

To use this integration you need create a project in Google Developers Console.

Step 2: Create Project

Click on Create Project button. A popup should appear asking for Project Name and Project ID. These are set by you and can be anything you’d like.

Step 3: Enable the Calendar API

Once the project has been created, go back to the Projects screen and select your project. Within your project, go to APIs & auth link on the left side menu. The menu will expand to provide you more options, click on the link for APIs. Locate Calendar API and enable it.

Step 4: Setup your Consent Screen options

Go to API’s & auth > Consent screen, now you only need fill the PRODUCT NAME field:

google-calendar-consent-screen

Note that you can use any name, but it is mandatory to fill this field.

Step 5: Create Credentials

Under the APIs & auth link menu on the left, click on Credentials. Then click on Create new Client ID.

Step 6: Insert New Credentials

Application type should be Web.

Authorized JavaScript Origins can be blank or your domain.

Set the Authorized redirect URI as http://example.com/wc-api/wc_bookings_google_calendar/. Where example.com is should be your domain.

Step 7: Copy Credentials to your WordPress settings

Navigate to WooCommerce > Settings> Integration > Google Calendar on your WordPress site. Copy and paste the credentials that were just provided to you from Google into your WordPress site. This will include your Client ID and Client Secret.

Step 8: Google Calendar ID

If you haven’t done so already, create the calendar you’ll be using in Google Calendar. Once your calendar is created, navigate to My calendars menu on the left and select Calendar Settings.

google_calendar_settings

On this screen you will see a section called Calendar Address. Copy the Calendar ID provided to you and insert it into your WooCommerce settings from Step 6 under Calendar ID. Now press Save.

Step 9: A

I tried yelling at the computer for a while with no luck, so I prayed to Google and was blessed.. for a $15 tithe.

Inline image 2

this plugin will handle exhibitor WP registration before the sale, allowing for reg activation for press & vip to be controlled separately.. 

user meta pro will handle reg / emails / activation for press / vip / platinum

woocommerce and the aforementioned plugin with handle everything for exhibitors

so I had to deconstruct the exhibitor reg, rebuild and test.. the emails are still being sorted / styled but everything is good from a work flow vantage.

long story short, im slightly behind where I was yesterday at this time but with less hair.

911-WebDev hourly - 04/24/2015 - mobile friendly go

sen:
	without the viewport (mobile screen) set
	
	with it set to ‘device-width’
	
	however because the site is not responsive it basically only loads the top left 10% of the site because thats what setting the viewport does.. which google is requiring.. so viewers will have to pinch / zoom out
Mike Fournier:
	That's because of device with?
sen:
	yeah.. if no viewport size is set, it loads the full site but small, and also google barks about the font size
	small = zoomed out / full screen
Mike Fournier:
	So we are stuck right now?
sen:
	well according to google if I leave it like this you’re good but its a UX/UI no-no technically.. not the worst in the world
Dave:
	I don't get it the site seem to be working just great from my iPhone

MHS WordPress™ development - 01/14/2015 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: hey Jan, yeah it was quiet the buildout.. about 8 months all tolled, was expecting 3. 

the homepage layout was totally changed from scroll style to full page (initially they wanted borders and I had to fight to get them away from that idea as it is not 2002)
the staff template was changed several times
we switched all the main slider images after I had worked them in Photoshop. (went from people / exercise pics to all nature)
which reminds me I didnt charge for any stock photos either, which was probably another $100 but no worries, just a note
on my end, I learned of a new / better technique midway thru that allows total template-ing of the website. literally nothing can be screwed up by the client and everything is completely editable. it blows away the MHS sites in terms of ease of use. it required rebuilding of several templates and the admin. this is why Im ok with giving a discount like it did.
most of my communication of overages was with Greg as we gchatted most days
mobile device testing / design is def included in the estimate but its one of those things that is always done at the end and if the budgets up its hard to justify spending a lot of time on it if theres no more $

I run timers on everything I do, its the only way I can stay sane. We're up to 90+ clients including the largest and most global art fair in the world, scope-art.com that didn't happen by accident. We're cranking out hugely customized WordPress™ website for really cheap. We have taken over 3 different WordPress™ websites in the past 2 years that cost more than 150k combined to develop and they look just like what CARE ended up with (mostly because I learned these techniques from these expensive sites). My initial estimate may have been on the light side but the end product is perfect.

WCM-WordPress - 08/05/2014 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: hi Meghann, so what you did was create a wordpress.com site vs a wordpress.org site, which is what I created for ams

the major differences are: 

wordpress.com is hosted by WordPress™ and is highly limited (it is not usually for professional companies), you do not own the content, you cannot access the source files or add plugins (I believe, haven't used it years).

wordpress.org is simply the homepage for the organization that develops the actual WordPress application. They produce and support it for free and allow it to be completely manipulated (known as opensource / freeware). WordPress.org is essentially where they allow you to download the same files that you just used to make http://waldorfcapitalmgt.com/ with the main difference being that you have to provide your own hosting however the final product is your intellectual property & modifiable.

if you look at your login page you can see what I mean.

Inline image 1

thing.thisthing.com = thisthing.com ownership
thisthing.thing.com = thing.com ownership

Seeing as this is a completely new site/endeavor (correct??) traffic will likely be light at first. I can let you just piggyback onto your AMS hosting account for an additional 150 a year and then I can set everything up properly. 

Since your hosting account is already setup, I can have the splash page up quick but I will also need to setup the dns with wherever you purchased that domain from, as well as any email you might have already created. 

Generally once I have a working relationship with a client I appreciate just tracking hours. I use harvestapp.com (could be useful for legal, lmk I have referral links, free to try) which times/logs everything I do, it even has an idle timer so if I step away from my computer, that time is subtracted when I return. I try to keep my hourly at 80-100 per hour. 

To set up a new WordPress™ install / database, change dns settings (so the domain points to my servers) and setup a few pages we're talking 5-6 hours max a
WCM-WordPress - 08/10/2014 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: install worpdress setup hosting  !!! ADD BILL FOR HOSTING !!!!

WCM-WordPress - 08/12/2014 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: setup like baincapital / set dns / splash page online / setup waldorfcapitalmanagement.com / install plugin deployment package / setup baseline background image rotation / I would highly recommend purchasing http://waldorfcapitalmanagement.com/ (I just did to be safe, I get paranoid just mentioning/checking if they are available as ive seen them drop off before) 

you can still use the mgt domain for email/biz cards etc (website will redirect) but I would recommend setting up the website at management.com as your SEO will be much better, mgt vs management is pretty huge.

WCM-WordPress - 08/13/2014 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: reset dns to WordPress.com / edit wordpress.com dns to make site live / cool I got in and added the appropriate record, just the website should show up now and the mail will be untouched. its kind of a weird setup now because you're basically just using wordpress.com to host your nameservers when usually those would be at your web host or domain provider. it just kind of like an extra / unnecessary setup in you're not using WordPress.com

I have the MX info now so tonight I can set it up properly at your godaddy account tonight and ditch WordPress.com. Just do a mail test first thing in the AM but this def shouldnt cause any problems.

MX	waldorfcapitalmgt.com	mail handled by waldorfcapitalmgt.com.inbound10.mxlogic.net. with priority 10
MX	waldorfcapitalmgt.com	mail handled by waldorfcapitalmgt.com.inbound10.mxlogicmx.net. with priority 10

https://wordpress.org/plugins/wp-retina-2x/

I haven't even done it on my site yet bc it caused like 35 image / size problems when I activated it. basically if you have resolution to spare (ie wordpress is rendering an image at less than 100% of its size, either from html or css constraint), this plugin will take advantage of it. If not, the same image but of a higher resolution needs to be found / created to replace. Currently Retina is essentially double the standard web resolution of 72dpi.

its not going away I can tell you that it just depends if you give a damn.. ive only had it on my laptop for couple months and I'll say, you might not notice when its not done, but you definitely notice when it is...

MHS Web Design - 09/03/2014 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: Hey Erin,

responses in bold
 
Re: RSVP forms. I see what you're hoping to do. Unfortunately the Overlook sales team is not as familiar with the website content as you or we are. So giving them a page headline will not make it clear at a glance what folks are signing up for. They might find it helpful if the email referenced the landing page stub, as they have copies of print advertising and direct mail referencing those URLs in front of them. But even that asks them to take an extra step and we know they have very, very little time to cross reference.

I can include the page url info as well, pretty much any info can be added, I just create a custom field on the page admin and pass that field data to the confirmation / notification email
 
Also, we will frequently promote multiple events on one page so I'd rather not eliminate event selection - that's critical info for the sales team. It needs to be clear for the site visitor and their choice needs to be specified/broken out in the emails. SAE VIP-SF - 08/25/2014 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: reviewing doc

Hey James, so I reviewed your doc, thank you for being so specific it really helps. 

overview (if im understanding): 

This is an advanced user registration form config however it is a little tricky as there are 5 major things happening on submit. 

1. advanced user registration form / WordPress™ account creation (extra fields added with Rocketgeek / Eventbrite integration)
2. user profile page creation (with public url?)
3. EventBrite registration / payment process
4. Salesforce API user data push
5. Constant Contact mailing list add (optional for user)

* Need to consider a basic event reg form as well, for logged-in users so they don't have to enter data twice or at least pre-populate form data for them.

After that it is the User Interface config which is independent from the registration / form (also fairly standard comparatively) and I can break that down some more but I wanted to get this email off to you first.

Let me know if I'm on the right track and feel free to call me whenever, Im working the rest of today - 8pm. Tmrw I am in and out of meetings all morning but will be available after 1-2
SAE VIP-SF - 08/29/2014 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: bid

SAE VIP-SF - 09/03/2014 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: responses in bold!

Thanks again for the proposal. Everything looks good more or less. I think we need to chat through the two forms mentioned - I'm not sure that our plan necessitates two separate forms. If it does, I think the normal 'apply' for VIP status should be on a separate linked page. 

this makes life easier, its just one of those things that you have to plan for. If user account creation and event registration can be separate then only one new form would be needed, I was just concerned with requesting duplicate data for those already registered.

I wanted to explore the conversation further about the user account creation and what our options might be in terms of utilizing the same 'Press User' backend. That system allows Press to register with a set of information, be approved and have their account activated, then login to a 'Press area' which allows them access to items that are otherwise hidden. 

Yes this is running off the wp-users / Rocketgeek plugin. Within being logged I cannot say for sure but it should be easy enough to have a 'VIP User' admin backend and keep everything similar. It will probably just a be a slightly different registration form.

I wonder if we had all our VIPs that we currently only snail mail loaded into that wp-user database that someone who received a card in the mail could access their account with a last name and zip code, or something similar? 

this would just require a 'first-time user' creation we can handle that by sending out a dynamic mass email to all VIP requesting them to create an account. if you have email addresses for them we could pre-populate the username with that info and they would just need to create a password from there.. 

how many VIPs are there? It may just be easier to have a quick script written to transfer all the data to WP and use randomly generated passwords.

I'm available today if you want to jump on the phone and chat it out a bit? I know this would be a more complex system so the propos

ER - General Web - 10/16/2014 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: After 6 hours of combing through the website code completely, 
we have optimized the it to reduce load time

What was optimized? It looks like WordPress™ was updated which the client should have been informed of in advance. WordPress™ updates can often cause problems with themes and plugins and need to be closely monitored, especially with this theme as it has been finicky in the past

 and also fixed some code that was not accurate for mobile and if it wasn’t fixed it could have 
allow hackers to access site code and ultimately corrupt the website.

as far as I know, code that is 'not accurate for mobile' is not accurate for anything. mobile issues, at least in this environment/website, I would safely say are 100% style sheet based and as far as I know there is no way to access/corrupt a website via a .css (cascading style sheet) file.

MHS Web Design - 06/25/2014 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: Greg, I was in the middle of drafting this email in response to the one from Jessica tonight when I had a couple concerns.

My initial concern was that they are having difficulty adding a tracking script to a standard WordPress™ website. This is pretty basic stuff and I have given them all the info needed to do it themselves.

My MAJOR concern was when I googled the base url for this script in question
 
script async src="http://i.simpli.fi/dpx.jscid=13242&conversion=20&campaign_id=0&m=1"/script 

if you search for i.simpli.fi (the src base url) the results are frightening.

initially I thought maybe the initial email caught a virus that hijacked urls but then I looked at the actual site (3rd in the list when searching the root url. you have to be really shitty to have your site not come up 1st when you search for it exactly) but it appears to be a legitimate (looking) marketing co.

My question is, why are they associating with a company that has that much negative press & questionable tactics when there are many that do it legitimately? My guess is they have no idea. This service may produce results but there are certainly companies out there where the first 9 of 10 search results aren't reporting viruses, etc. Off the top of my head I would think http://www.hubspot.com/ would be the first thing to try.

Going off of my past interactions with CR, this Simpli nonsense, and a general disdain for marketing co's I think you might be being taken for a ride. I seriously question how a marketing co with multiple employees/offices cannot figure out how to add a single line / script to the biggest CMS in the world and allows their employees to use font styling in their emails. thats a personal caveat possibly but still not appropriate for any real co.

PLS advise as I put that email response on hold and certainly did not add that script to the website

MHS Web Design - 07/03/2014 - Web Design Hourly / Thomas Senecal: The script in question is connecting on the site and has been. They have complete access to the WordPress™ admin as well as all the source files (FTP info was sent, which allows edits to the functions.php file where the script should be enqueued). The way they are loading the script (via WordPress™ footer widgets) is not at all proper (widgets are for text/images/widgets). In WordPress™ scripts are loaded properly via the wp_enqueue_script function however the way they are adding it will still produce the same results.

proof the script is loading:

the same script is being called 3 times, this is a pass/fail scenario, calling it more than once is inappropriate and could cause conflicts. 

The script is most likely faulty / missing because when I try to connect to the script link directly http://i.simpli.fi/dpx.jscid=13242&conversion=20&campaign_id=0&m=1 it 404s / breaks no matter how I edit / trim the url.

I also just took a look at the widgets in WordPress™ and noticed they have recently inserted the same script into each (3x) footer widget. 

This is just poor procedure / understanding. Not only is it not the right location but the logic is completely lacking. The site shouldn't (attempt to) call a script 2 more times simply because it didn't work the first time (even though it is connecting, its just not working). If it was for testing purposes, then they should have removed them by now as its well past regular business hours...

Greg, with all respect, unless they can prove otherwise (several passes on Google™ have not yielded anything positive), I can not (call it the Hippocratic Oath of web design) add this malware script to any website, especially one on my servers. There are many marketing services that simply do not have these serious negative connotations.

the following is from the reputable site http://malwaretips.com/blogs/i-simpli-fi-removal/

I.simpli.fi is not a malicious domain itself, however this website is used
